exemplar.title: To what extent will launching an e-commerce website help improve NuTaste's financial position?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent will launching an e-commerce website help improve NuTaste's financial position?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear definition and development of the key concept of change
Consistent linkage of the concept in Ansoff analysis, payback discussion, and conclusion
weaknesses: Ratio analysis occasionally lacks explicit reference to the key concept
Definition of change is generic and unanchored in academic theory
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in band 4: it demonstrates strong understanding of the concept and a clear analysis with partial integration throughout. Occasional generic discussion and lack of seamless reintegration in certain sections prevent a band-5 rating.

strengths: Four supporting documents are provided, meeting the 3â5 requirement
Documents are clearly relevant to both quantitative tools and contextual discussion
weaknesses: Supporting documents lack page references or hyperlinks for easy traceability
Depth is limited to two years of accounts and survey findings are cited without full detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student has supplied a sufficient number of relevant documents (band 3) and they are in-depth enough to support the analysis without offering the broader range or exhaustive detail required for band 4.

strengths: Effective selection of three distinct tools (Ansoff Matrix, Payback Period, financial ratios)
Generally clear application of tools to the research question
weaknesses: Minor calculation inconsistencies and unclear currency notation weaken precision
Rationale for tool selection and document justification is underdeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Selection of tools merits band 4 due to its effectiveness, but application falls into band 3âadequately applied but with clarity issues and speculative assertionsâresulting in an overall band-3 rating for this criterion.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of multiple data sources (survey, cost projections, financial statements) supports thorough analysis
Most interpretations (e.g., payback feasibility, liquidity impact) are well linked to the research question
weaknesses: Comparative calculations (e.g., 2022 ratio steps) are sometimes omitted
Analytical integration is not sustained uniformly across all tools
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and use meet band-4 descriptors, and analysis is mostly effective with good integration. The absence of some detailed comparisons and trends prevents achieving the highest band.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
The research question is explicitly and directly answered
weaknesses: Conclusion section repeats descriptive content and lacks focus on unique insights
Recommendations could be streamlined to avoid redundancy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion satisfies both strands at the highest band (3), presenting a clear, evidence-based answer to the research question without contradictions.

strengths: Logical organization with a clear table of contents and coherent flow
Appropriate structure maintained throughout the report
weaknesses: Minor formatting glitches and inconsistent subheading use in the ToC
Could benefit from added subheadings (e.g., ratio analysis subsections) to enhance navigability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates an appropriate structure throughout (band 2). Few structural issues are present but do not impede overall coherence.

strengths: All required elements are present (title page, word count, references, documentation)
Academic integrity is clearly demonstrated via declaration and proper source acknowledgments
weaknesses: Inconsistent formatting (equations, citations, currency symbols) reduces clarity
Graphs and charts lack axis labels, units, and consistent styling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the highest band (2) by including all required elements, despite several minor issues in formatting and consistency.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure with a table of contents, section headings and coherent flow from introduction through tools to conclusion. Minor formatting glitches do not impede readability, matching âappropriate structure throughoutâ (band 2).

Each document (cost sheet, survey, financial statements, product portfolio) is clearly relevant to either the quantitative tools or the contextual discussion, and the commentary quotes figures from them. Depth is reasonable but not wide-ranging; for instance, only two years of accounts are used and the survey findings are cited but not presented in detail. This meets the descriptor for ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the extensive range required for band 4.

All required elementsâthe title page with word count, research question, methodology, analysis, conclusion, references and supporting document listâare present. Therefore the highest presentation band is awarded.

Four supporting documents are listed and attached, falling within the required 3â5 range. This satisfies the criteria for the middle and upper bands, so a band where 3 marks are awarded for 3â5 documents is selected.

Application is generally clear: Ansoff options are evaluated against survey data, payback is calculated from a cost sheet, and ratios are interpreted in relation to the proposed e-commerce change. Nevertheless, calculation explanations contain minor inconsistencies (e.g., contribution formula, unclear currency formatting) and some assertions are speculative. This fits ârelevance not always clear but adequately appliedâ (band 3).

The commentary draws on primary survey percentages, cost projections and two years of financial statement data. The data set is sufficiently broad to support analysis, though not exhaustive. Figures are generally used correctly, meeting the criterion of âsufficient selection and useâ (band 4).

Most analyses link results to the research questionâfor example, relating the 4.9-month payback to feasibility and showing how liquidity might improve with online cash inflows. Integration across tools is present but not sustained throughout; still, the analytical discussion is mostly effective, justifying band 4.

The student explicitly answers the research question, stating that launching an e-commerce website will improve NuTasteâs financial position and explaining why. This fulfils the descriptor for an explicit answer (band 3).

The student selects three distinct and appropriate toolsâAnsoff Matrix, Payback Period and key financial ratiosâcovering strategy, investment appraisal and financial performance. Together they provide multiple perspectives on the research question, demonstrating an effective rather than merely adequate selection.

The conclusion synthesises findings from all tools and is logically derived from the evidence presented; no contradictory claims are made. This satisfies âconclusions fully consistent with evidence,â the highest band for this strand.

NuTasteâs background, market position, product prices and detailed financial statements are woven into each tool. The Ansoff matrix options, payback inputs and ratio calculations all rely on organisation-specific data drawn from the supporting documents. There is, however, occasional generic discussion (e.g., âidealâ liquidity ratios) that is not fully tied back to NuTaste. This constitutes strong analysis with partial integration rather than comprehensive integration, so band 4 is appropriate.

The student clearly defines the key concept of change and links it to the shift from a purely B2B model to a mixed B2B-B2C approach. Throughout the introduction, Ansoff analysis, payback discussion and conclusion the commentary repeatedly references how this change will affect revenue, costs and risk. However, sections such as the ratio analysis discuss figures with little or no explicit linkage back to the concept itself, so the integration is strong but not seamless. This aligns best with the descriptor for strong understanding and partial integration (band 4).

annotations.comment: The definition of âChangeâ is generic; the student should link this to IBâs conceptual framework or change management theories to deepen integration and analytical depth.
The definition of Change is simplistic and unreferenced; anchor it in academic literature or IB guidelines to show deeper understanding of the key concept.
Current ratio calculation only shows 2023; include the step-by-step calculation for 2022 for consistency and comparison.
Connect the change in current ratio directly to the e-commerce cash flow impact by estimating additional liquid assets from online sales.
Graphs lack axis labels and units; ensure all figures and charts include clear titles, legends, and axis descriptions for presentation quality.
Include interpretation of the Quick Ratioâs 2022 figure in the text to highlight the trend rather than only tabulating it.
The COD recommendation is practical, but assess possible operational costs, delivery risks, and default rates to provide balanced evaluation.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes tool findings, but it redundantly repeats descriptions. Streamline to emphasize unique insights and recommendations.
The clear declaration of authenticity and acknowledgment of sources demonstrates strong academic integrity and compliance with presentation requirements.
The table of contents is well organized with clear headings and page numbers, enhancing the navigability and coherence of the report.
Consider adding subheadings for the ratio analysis (e.g., profitability, liquidity) in the ToC to reflect the reportâs detailed structure.
The background describes net losses but lacks quantification; include specific figures from Supporting Document 3 to strengthen context and analytical precision.
Sources are listed but not justified; explain why two years of accounts and the chosen survey sample size provide sufficient depth and relevance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The rationale for selecting the Ansoff Matrix and Payback Period is stated but could be bolstered by linking each tool explicitly to aspects of the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Limitations mention excluded marketing methods but do not analyze their impact on findings; discuss how these omissions might affect validity of the conclusion.
The tool overview for Ansoff Matrix is clear, but the student should specify evaluation criteria (e.g., risk, revenue potential) drawn from survey data for each quadrant.
The Ansoff matrix table is comprehensive, but it lacks weighted scoring to objectively compare and rank growth strategies.
The discussion of market penetration correctly flags low growth, but should cite the specific survey percentage (>60%) here to reinforce the point quantitatively.
The paragraph begins mid-sentence and formatting is inconsistent; ensure all sentences are complete and styling matches previous sections for readability.
When introducing Payback Period, explicitly link back to the concept of change to reinforce how the quantitative tool evaluates the strategic shift.
The cost-of-investment line uses a Yen symbol (Â¥) instead of rupee (â¹); correct the currency unit to ensure clarity and relevance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Use consistent LaTeX formatting for equations, e.g., $$\mathrm{Payback}\ Period=\frac{Cost}{Contribution\ per\ month}$$ and include units for each variable.
The term âVariable Express_{12}â is unclear; revise to something like $$\frac{Total\ Revenue - Variable\ Costs}{12}$$ to clarify the contribution calculation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Good reflection on Payback Period limitations; consider also applying NPV analysis to assess long-term financial viability of the e-commerce investment.
In COGS calculation, finance cost and depreciation should not be included; these belong below gross profit. Reclassify expense items appropriately.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The 35.26% GPM appears inconsistent with earlier figures; verify calculation inputs for both years and recalculate to ensure accuracy.
Consider adding net profit margin (even if negative) alongside GPM to provide a fuller picture of profitability performance.
The suggestion to negotiate raw material costs is valid; strengthen this by estimating potential percentage savings and their effect on gross profit.
The report omits net profit margin due to loss; consider discussing an alternative ratio (e.g., loss-after-tax ratio) to analyze overall performance.
The table shows loss after tax figures; calculate the percentage change year-on-year to demonstrate deeper analytical insight.
Ensure numeric formatting is consistent; the investment amount appears as â\bar{Î¾}46.01,500â here but should match earlier stated â¹46,01,500.
References are inconsistently formatted; adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) to ensure uniformity across all entries.
Supporting documents are listed but lack page references or hyperlinks; include page numbers or referencing cues to enhance document traceability.
The Clark (Paul) citation omits publisher details for the OSC resource; complete each reference with author, title, publisher, and access date.
